subject: Chemistry
title: YInMn Blue: A New Blue Pigment
description: A quick, practical guide to YInMn Blue: A New Blue Pigment.

hook.text: In 2009, a chemistry student accidentally created the first new blue pigment in over 200 years. It's called YInMn blue.

buildup.text: Andrew Smith was heating yttrium, indium, and manganese oxides at Oregon State. The sample came out of the furnace vivid blue.

discovery.text: Manganese ions in the crystal absorb red and green light. The structure is so stable the color never fades.

twist.text: YInMn blue reflects infrared heat. Buildings painted with it stay cooler—it's a pigment that saves energy.

climax.text: Crayola added it as a crayon color in 2017 called 'Bluetiful.' A lab accident became a household name.

punchline.text: A lab accident at 1,200°C. The newest blue pigment in two centuries.

quiz.question: What unexpected property does YInMn blue have?
options: It reflects infrared heat, keeping painted surfaces cooler
It glows in the dark without any external energy
It changes color depending on temperature
